raw:
{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 3,
    "R1.7": 3,
    "RX.1": 1,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "Mentions ConfidNet and Trust Score but doesn't provide a detailed comparison with other relevant works like selective classifiers or self-evaluation methods in language models.",
    "R1.7": "Flags missing citations for prior work on confidence calibration and selective classification, but doesn't provide specific bibliographic details.",
    "RX.1": "Presents all claims as equally confident without acknowledging uncertainty or limitations.",
    "RX.3": "Mostly stays within the scope of improving confidence calibration in ViTs, but could benefit from a more explicit discussion of its limitations and potential biases.",
    "RX.4": "Doesn't introduce any new errors or harmful content, but lacks analysis on potential regressions compared to existing methods.",
    "RX.5": "Describes the rewrite process but doesn't provide specific annotations or traceability for the changes made."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.5
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: Mentions ConfidNet and Trust Score but doesn't provide a detailed comparison with other relevant works like selective classifiers or self-evaluation methods in language models.
rationale.R1.7: Flags missing citations for prior work on confidence calibration and selective classification, but doesn't provide specific bibliographic details.
rationale.RX.1: Presents all claims as equally confident without acknowledging uncertainty or limitations.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly stays within the scope of improving confidence calibration in ViTs, but could benefit from a more explicit discussion of its limitations and potential biases.
rationale.RX.4: Doesn't introduce any new errors or harmful content, but lacks analysis on potential regressions compared to existing methods.
rationale.RX.5: Describes the rewrite process but doesn't provide specific annotations or traceability for the changes made.
